other_observers: Loch Kilpatrick accompanied me when the bird was discovered. Seen by dozens, probably hundreds of birders during it's 2.5 week stay.; I am the original finder, Loch Kilpatrick and Joey Kellner assited in identifying.
duration: 3 hours during the original observation, seen for many hours after.
specific_location: Chatfield Reservoir, both sides. Originally found on the marina sandspit (Douglas Co.)
description: A large gull, Herring Gull sized but bulkier, as seen next to several Ring-billed Gulls and a California Gull. The bird had a dark mantle (about the shade of Lesser Black-backed Gull), white head, neck, breast and underparts, a large yellow bill with a bulbous tip and small red spot at the gonys. The primaries were extensively black with a small, square shaped mirror on P10 and small white tips on P10, P9, P8, P7, P6 and P5. P7, P6 and P5 also showed much less black at the tip with small, white sub-terminal spots. P5 had a large white tip with a small black mark in the center. The rest of the primaries and all of the secondaries showed a large white tip on an otherwise dark grey (mantled-colored) feather. This gave the appearance of a thick white line across the trailing edge of the wing, seen in flight. The tail was white, the eye was darkish, about the color of a Mew Gull's iris. A bright yellow orbital ring circled the eye. The legs were pink, as was the gape as seen in the open mouth. In the sitting bird, there was a large, white tertial crescent, and a broad tertial skirt.Behaviors: The bird was seen preening, flying, feeding, resting and swimming. It appeared to have an injured left leg. It did not stand or put pressure on the left leg, but was seen using it's left leg to scratch it's face. The bird was seen flying around the lake, roosting at 1 of about 4 different locations.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek
